text.title: TypeScript Strict Mode — Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules every typescript-strict-mode application must obey.

rule.rationale: Single flag turns on the canonical strict family (noImplicitAny, strictNullChecks, etc.).

rule.statement: Enable noUncheckedIndexedAccess, exactOptionalPropertyTypes, noImplicitReturns, noFallthroughCasesInSwitch on top of strict.
rule.rationale: These four close the remaining 'silently wrong' loopholes that strict alone misses.

rule.statement: Validate every IO boundary (HTTP body, queue message, env vars) with Zod schemas. Use Schema.parse for trusted input, .safeParse for untrusted.
rule.rationale: TypeScript types vanish at runtime; Zod is the runtime layer that ensures the type-level contract.

rule.statement: Replace any with unknown. Narrow with type guards before use.
rule.rationale: any disables type checking on every chained operation; unknown forces explicit narrowing.

rule.statement: Never use bare // @ts-ignore. Always // @ts-expect-error[code] // reason: why.
rule.rationale: Bare ignores hide unknown bugs; expect-error fails build if the error goes away (preventing rot).

rule.statement: Export-visible functions declare explicit return types.
rule.rationale: Internal inference is fine; explicit at the boundary prevents inferred type drift breaking consumers.
